exercises.translation: Summer vacation is six weeks long.

wrong_choice_explanations.週: 週 indicates specific weeks (like 'this week') — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about duration, not which weeks.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because six days would be too short for summer vacation.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because six hours would be impossibly short for summer vacation.

exercises.example_sentence: 日本に三__滞在しました。
exercises.translation: I stayed in Japan for three weeks.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because three days would be a very short stay in Japan.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, three months would be a much longer stay than what's typically described this way.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because three hours is not a meaningful duration for staying in a country.

exercises.translation: I was absent for two weeks due to illness.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while possible, two days would be a relatively short illness absence.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because you don't typically measure illness absence in hours.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because two years would be an extremely long illness absence.

exercises.example_sentence: この仕事は四__で終わります。
exercises.translation: This work will finish in four weeks.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while four days is possible, it suggests a much shorter project than what's typically described this way.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, four months would be a much longer project timeline.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because four hours would be too short for substantial work projects.

exercises.translation: The trip is planned for one week.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while one day trips exist, it would be quite short for what's described as a planned trip.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because one hour is not a meaningful duration for a trip.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, one month would be a much longer trip than what's typically described this way.

exercises.example_sentence: プロジェクトに五__かかりました。

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while five days is possible, it would be quite short for a substantial project.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, five months would be a much longer project duration.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because five years would be extremely long for most projects.

exercises.translation: The training continues for three weeks.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while three days of training is possible, it would be quite short for comprehensive training.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because three hours would be too short for meaningful training programs.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, three months would be much longer than typical training programs.

exercises.translation: The hospitalization was for two weeks.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while two days of hospitalization is possible, it would be quite short for most medical conditions.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, two months would be a much longer hospitalization.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because hospitalizations are not typically measured in hours.

exercises.example_sentence: コースは八__かかります。

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because eight days would be too short for most educational courses.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, eight months would be much longer than typical short courses.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because courses are not typically measured in total hours this way.

exercises.example_sentence: 出張で四__東京にいます。
exercises.translation: I'm in Tokyo for four weeks on a business trip.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — while four days business trips exist, it would be relatively short for what's described this way.
wrong_choice_explanations.ヶ月: ヶ月 means months — while possible, four months would be an unusually long business trip assignment.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because four years would be more like a job transfer than a business trip.
